name: engram-mcp
description: Universal AI memory — store, search, and recall memories with semantic similarity and knowledge graph traversal.
long_description:
Engram gives any AI model persistent, structured memory across sessions.

On first launch it installs `@engram-ai-memory/mcp` from npm (requires Node.js ≥ 22 and npm). The embedding model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) downloads automatically on first use (~25 MB).

**18 tools across 7 categories:**
- **Memory** — `store_memory`, `search_memory`, `recall_context`, `add_knowledge`, `forget`
- **Stats** — `memory_stats`, `index_status`, `embedding_status`
- **Lifecycle** — `decay_sweep`, `decay_policy`, `re_embed`
- **Contradictions** — `check_contradictions`, `resolve_contradiction`
- **Tags** — `list_tags`, `tag_memory`
- **Webhooks** — `webhook_subscribe`, `webhook_list`
- **Plugins** — `plugin_list`

**Recommended workflow:**
```
Session start  → recall_context(task description)
During work    → store_memory(decisions, findings)
End of session → store_memory(session summary)
```

All embeddings run 100% locally — no external API calls.

tools.name: store_memory
tools.description: Store an episodic, semantic, or procedural memory

tools.name: recall_context
tools.description: Assemble formatted context for AI prompts (primary recall tool)

tools.name: add_knowledge
tools.description: Add a semantic fact or concept

tools.name: index_status
tools.description: Vector index status and performance info

tools.name: embedding_status
tools.description: Embedding model status and re-embed needs

tools.name: decay_sweep
tools.description: Run memory decay based on Ebbinghaus forgetting curve

tools.name: re_embed
tools.description: Re-embed memories with the current model

tools.name: resolve_contradiction
tools.description: Resolve a contradiction using a strategy

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: Get tag cloud or memories by tag

tools.name: tag_memory
tools.description: Add or remove a tag on a memory

tools.name: webhook_subscribe
tools.description: Subscribe a URL to memory events (HMAC-signed)

db_path.title: Database Path
db_path.description: Path to the SQLite file where memories are stored. Leave empty to use the default (~/.engram/engram.db).

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: Optional namespace to isolate memories per project or context. Leave empty for the global namespace.
